evals.question_short: Row-level TTL — could PG support per-row auto-expire?

assertions.text: Output follows the 8-section structure (Problem / Why / Subsystems / Has-this-been-tried / Approaches / Recommend / DECISIONs / NOT-figured-out)
Names ≥2 relevant subsystem docs from knowledge/subsystems/*
Sketches at least 2 genuinely distinct approaches (not 3 flavors of the same)
Each candidate approach has explicit Pros + Cons + Scope (small/medium/large)
Names existing prior art (pg_partman / pg_cron / Timescale / Citus, or CommitFest search result) AND in §4 explicitly uses the out-of-tree-extensions category from the skill's Edit-2
Consults the scenarios layer and names ≥1 scenario plus uses the composite-features pattern (lists 2+ scenarios it composes)
Picks ONE recommended approach with a reason
Surfaces ≥3 DECISION: questions only the user can answer
Includes a 'What this brainstorm did NOT figure out' section drawing the boundary with Phase 2
Stays under ~300 lines AND does NOT drop file:line citations into source/
Tags uncertain claims as [unverified] (or equivalent honesty marker)
Applies the distinctness test from §Method step 4 (explicit or implicit: approaches differ on subsystem / invariant / surface)

evals.question_short: Online rewind to LSN X without restart

assertions.text: Output follows the 8-section structure
Names ≥2 relevant subsystems including access-transam and either replication or storage-buffer
Identifies existing PITR / recovery_target_lsn machinery as prior art
Identifies pg_rewind as an existing tool (and notes it's offline)
Sketches at least 2 genuinely distinct approaches
Each candidate has Pros + Cons + Scope
Notes replication-slot semantics as a hard problem under rewind
Picks ONE recommended approach with reasoning + names what would have to be true for the others to win
Surfaces ≥3 DECISION: questions
Flags scenarios-layer gap ('no scenario covers modifying the live recovery state machine') with the heuristic 'recurring change-class'
Stays under ~300 lines AND does not drop file:line cites
Names ≥1 commercial / fork prior art (Neon / Aurora) when discussing the branchable-DB approach — uses the broadened §4 lens from Edit-2

evals.question_short: plpgsql_check as core — dead-code analysis at CREATE FUNCTION time

assertions.text: IDENTIFIES that the `plpgsql_check` extension already exists in §4 under the out-of-tree-extensions category (Edit-2)
Reframes the brainstorm as 'upstream vs make-extension-easier vs move-to-contrib' rather than designing from scratch
Names the existing `plpgsql_validator` hook in `pl_handler.c` and the `check_function_bodies` GUC as the existing surface
Sketches at least 2 distinct approaches that respect the prior-art reframe
Each candidate has Pros + Cons + Scope
Names ≥1 relevant scenario from `_index.md` (e.g. #30, #21) or flags a gap with the recurrence heuristic
Picks ONE recommendation with reasoning + names conditions for the others to win
FIRST DECISION: explicitly surfaces the extension-already-exists reframe (per Anti-patterns 'extension-already-exists' rule + §Output 7 worked example)
Surfaces ≥3 DECISION: questions total
Stays under ~300 lines AND does not drop file:line cites
Output follows the 8-section structure
Avoids the 'Designing instead of brainstorming' anti-pattern — no enumeration of catalog columns / SQLSTATEs / test files

summary.note: iter-2 rubric is stricter than iter-1: 3 new assertions added (distinctness-test invocation in E1, commercial-prior-art for E2, anti-pattern-avoidance + FIRST-DECISION reframe in E3) to probe the new SKILL.md content. with_skill still saturates; baseline gained 1 hit (anti-pattern-avoidance in E3 is met by any non-Phase-2-style answer).
